ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Continued To Aug-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Phillip Campbell, Esq. appearing representing Ida Sadler, administrator and successor trustee.
Ray Robinson, Esq. appearing representing Josie Carter, trust beneficiary.

The following matters were called together:

1) PTR-20-303765, Joanne Anderson Revocable Trust; and
2) PES-22-305372, Estate of Queenie Anderson

This matter comes on calendar being set for the following:

A. Petition Concerning the Internal Affairs of a Trust and for Orders, including: 1)Instructing Trustee and Compelling Account; 2) Directing Return of Trust Assets; 3) Order for Blocked Account; 4) Removing Trustee and Appointing Successor; 5) Suspending Trustee; 6) Compelling Redress of Breach of Trust; 7) Damages; 8) Allocating of Costs and Disgorgement; 9) Imposition of Constructive Trust; 10) Prohibiting Respondent from Using Trust Assets
B. Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account; Final Distribution; for Allowance of Compensation for Statutory Administrator Fees and Allowance of Compensation of Statutory and Extraordinary Fees and Costs

Parties referred to and ordered to complete voluntary mediation program under Breslin. BRESLIN ORDER TO BE ISSUED. Parties to submit a proposed order that is specific about the issues that will be mediated and includes selected mediator.

Amended Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account; Final Distribution; for Allowance of Compensation for Statutory Administrator Fees and Allowance of Compensation of Statutory and Extraordinary Fees and Costs filed May-29-2026.
Hearing on petition is continued to Aug-17-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Parties are ordered to complete mediation by Aug-17-2026. The parties shall submit a joint case management statement as to the status of mediation on or before Aug-10-2026.

Hearing on Petition Concerning the Internal Affairs of a Trust and for Orders, including: 1)Instructing Trustee and Compelling Account; 2) Directing Return of Trust Assets; 3) Order for Blocked Account; 4) Removing Trustee and Appointing Successor; 5) Suspending Trustee; 6) Compelling Redress of Breach of Trust; 7) Damages; 8) Allocating of Costs and Disgorgement; 9) Imposition of Constructive Trust; 10) Prohibiting Respondent from Using Trust Assets is continued to Aug-17-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called for hearing together:
1) PTR-20-303765, Joanne Anderson Revocable Trust; and
2) PES-22-305372, Estate of Queenie Anderson.

Appearances:
Ray Robinson for trust beneficiary Josie Carter (via Zoom).
Phillip Campbell for administrator and successor trustee Ida Sadler (via Zoom).

At the outset, the Court inquired whether the Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account filed in the estate matter would resolve the trust issues. Mr. Campbell reported that the matters remained dependent on receipt of a property tax bill from the San Francisco Tax Collector and completion of federal and state tax returns, which would determine available funds for distribution. Mr. Campbell further stated that disputes existed between the parties regarding allocation of proceeds from jointly owned real property and identification of heirs, and requested a ninety-day continuance while awaiting the tax information.

The Court stated that the matters should proceed through case management.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matters to June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 and scheduled a Case Management Conference to occur on the same date. The Court directed the parties to submit a joint case management statement identifying the issues to be litigated, regardless of whether tax information had been received. The Court stated that a case management order would issue.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-07-2024 Continued To Jun-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Fenton appeared by zoom for Ida Sadler (administrator).

Mr. Fenton stated that he filed a report and an inventory and appraisal/re-appraisal for sale, that he is not sure why there has been a delay and that he believes they have addressed the Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Fenton also stated that he may have to amend the report because he believes a prayer was not included and that a proposed order was filed yesterday. The court informed Mr. Fenton that it had considered suspending the administrator if the issues remained uncleared. The Report of Administration is continued to June 11, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jones. Not reported.

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-02-2024 Continued To May-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Fenton appeared by zoom for Ida Sadler.

Mr. Fenton requested 30-days. The Report on Status of Administration is continued to May 7, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Fenton is to have his papers filed one week prior.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ray Robinson appeared via zoom for Joise Carter (requestor).
Phillip Cambell appeared via zoom for Joanne Anderson.

Mr. Cambell stated that there are issues with the title, that it will take 6 months to clear up title, and that then it will allow them to sell the property. Status on close of estate is continued to February 13, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bernardo. Not reported.
